sourceMovie.title: The Memory of a Killer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Memory of a Killer (2003) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Memory of a Killer clicked for you, John Wick: Chapter 2 (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Memory of a Killer?
faq.answer: Try John Wick: Chapter 2, Memory, The Killer—each shares DNA with The Memory of a Killer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Memory of a Killer?
faq.answer: The Memory of a Killer (2003) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Memory of a Killer part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Vincke & Verstuyft trilogy" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
